ovulation.title: Ovulation Calculator
ovulation.description: Calculate your ovulation date and fertile window based on your cycle length.
ovulation.howItWorksText: This calculator uses the calendar method: ovulation occurs approximately 14 days before your next period. For a 28-day cycle, that is day 14; for a 32-day cycle, day 18. The fertile window spans 5 days before ovulation, ovulation day, and 1 day after.

faq.a: About 14 days before the next period. In a 28-day cycle, day 14; in a 32-day cycle, day 18. Source: ACOG.
The 5 days before ovulation plus the day of ovulation itself. Sperm survive up to 5 days in the uterus; the egg lives 12–24 hours after release.

faq.q: How accurate is the calendar method?
faq.a: Reasonably reliable for regular cycles (±2 days). For irregular cycles, LH ovulation tests or basal body temperature tracking are more accurate.
Clear, stretchy cervical mucus (egg-white texture), slight rise in basal body temperature (0.2–0.5 °C), mid-cycle pelvic pain, and increased libido.

faq.q: Can this calculator be used for contraception?
faq.a: No. The calendar method has a Pearl index of 9–40 and is not adequate as a sole contraceptive. Symptothermal (FAM) methods are safer.
Highest 1–2 days before ovulation (~33 % per cycle per Wilcox 1995). Intercourse every 1–2 days during the fertile window maximizes the chance.
